p1_1a_1|ENV.summary: First of three words associated with the term 'older adult' (open-ended, first mention).

p2|ENV.summary: Number of household members aged 60 years and older.

p3|ENV.summary: Whether older household members are family relations.

p6_1|ENV.summary: Agreement that a person becomes old when needing help with most daily activities.

p7_1a_1|ENV.summary: Primary problem faced by older adults in Mexico (first mention, open-ended).

p7_2a_1|ENV.summary: Second most important problem faced by older adults in Mexico (open-ended).

p8|ENV.summary: Primary responsibility for older adult care in Mexico (opinion on who should be responsible).

p9|ENV.summary: Perceived change in overall situation of older adults in Mexico since 2010 (improved or worsened).

p10|ENV.summary: Expected change in overall situation of older adults in Mexico in 10 years (better or worse).

p11|ENV.summary: Concern about the possible increase in population aged 60+ in Mexico (worry scale).

p12|ENV.summary: Opinion on how much older adults' opinions should be considered in family decisions.

p13_1|ENV.summary: Agreement that health of people aged 60+ is poor (agree-disagree scale).

p14_1|ENV.summary: Agreement that people lose interest in things as they age (agree-disagree scale).

p15_1|ENV.summary: Perception of media image of older adults on radio (positive or negative).

p16_1|ENV.summary: Belief that negligence (failure to meet needs) is a type of abuse against older adults.

p17_1|ENV.summary: Opinion on how much abuse toward older adults depends on their age.

p18_1|ENV.summary: Belief that older adults are discriminated against by age in the workplace.

p19_1|ENV.summary: Knowledge of physical abuse (being hit) experienced by an older adult family member.

p20|ENV.summary: Knowledge of measures to prevent abuse toward older adults (yes/no).

p20aa_1|ENV.summary: Specific measures to prevent abuse toward older adults (open-ended follow-up).

p21_2|ENV.summary: Adequacy of psychological care services for older adults in Mexico (adequate-inadequate scale).

p22|ENV.summary: Desire to continue working after reaching pension eligibility age (yes/no).

p24_1a_1|ENV.summary: First of three reasons why older adults continue working after obtaining pension (open-ended).

p25_1|ENV.summary: Agreement that older adults should have equal employment rights as young people (agree-disagree).

p26|ENV.summary: Opinion on justification for denying employment to an older adult capable of doing the job.

p27|ENV.summary: Perception of employment opportunities available to older adults in Mexico.

p29_1|ENV.summary: Opinion on older adults' contribution to childcare of grandchildren.

p30|ENV.summary: Perception of adult children's willingness to care for their aging parents.

p31|ENV.summary: Opinion on the quality of care adult children provide to aging parents today.

p32_1|ENV.summary: Availability of sidewalks with handrails for older adults in respondent's neighborhood.

p33|ENV.summary: Presence of accessibility installations for older adults in respondent's home or building.

p34_1|ENV.summary: Perceived need for mobility structures (sidewalks, handrails, ramps) for older adults in neighborhood.

p35|ENV.summary: Willingness to have taxes used to adapt public spaces for older adult accessibility and mobility.

p36|ENV.summary: Opinion on whether older adults in Mexico have a dignified life (yes/no).

p37_1|ENV.summary: Primary limitation to quality of life for older adults in Mexico (first mention, open-ended).

p38|ENV.summary: Perception of how much people consider the problems of older adults today.

p39|ENV.summary: Opinion on importance of older adults' problems to Mexican society.

p40_1|ENV.summary: Importance of economic aid as a public policy to help older adults in Mexico (0-10 scale).

p41_1|ENV.summary: Opinion on whether organizations of older adults and NGOs play a positive or negative role in addressing aging challenges in Mexico.

p42_1|ENV.summary: Agreement that the State should be responsible for care and attention of older adults (agree-disagree).

p43|ENV.summary: Preference for type of care arrangement if an older adult family member needed help.

p44|ENV.summary: Willingness to contribute financially if an older adult family member needed care.

p45_1|ENV.summary: First of three most important reasons for helping older adults (open-ended).

p46_1|ENV.summary: Frequency of visiting one's parents (behavioral measure of intergenerational contact).
